summary: A medieval-style castle or fortification structure featuring four tall brick towers with overhanging flat roofs, connected by lower brick walls with white fence railings. The central area contains a raised platform with glass windows and internal structures. The building is symmetrically arranged along a central axis with a rectangular footprint.

shape.overall_form: rectangular with four corner towers and central raised platform
shape.symmetry: bilateral symmetry along central north-south axis

materials.name: brick
materials.role: primary wall material for towers and walls

rebuild_hints: Build four tall brick towers at corners, each approximately 2-3 blocks wide and 10-12 blocks tall
Add overhanging wooden roof structures on each tower using stairs or slabs extending 2-3 blocks outward
Connect towers with brick walls approximately 3-4 blocks tall
Install white fence railings along the outer perimeter at mid-height of connecting walls
Create central raised platform with glass windows and internal structures
Maintain bilateral symmetry along the north-south axis
Use dark brick (appears to be dark red/brown) for primary construction
Add foundation blocks (lighter colored) at base level around entire perimeter
Include small internal courtyard or plaza area in center
uncertainties: Exact tower dimensions and internal structure unclear from exterior views
Specific block types for roof overhangs (could be stairs, slabs, or full blocks)
Internal layout and purpose of central platform not fully visible
Exact height of connecting walls relative to towers
Whether structure is completely hollow or has internal supports
Precise arrangement of glass windows and internal features
